intent: 3000-word guide: 'the grocery aisle as pharmacology 101'. (1) One Stop Market grocery-gourmet-food AND health-household — pick 8 products that claim a health benefit (probiotic, antioxidant, electrolyte, vitamin-fortified, etc.). Name, price, rating, claim. (2) Reddit — /f/AskScience AND /f/Nutrition (or substitute) — 5+ posts on whether those claims hold up. (3) Wikipedia — 'Probiotic', 'Antioxidant', 'Electrolyte'. 5+ facts on evidence base. The report must: (a) score each product claim on evidence-strength (1-5), (b) identify 2 empty marketing claims, (c) identify 2 actually-evidence-backed picks, (d) close with a 'minimum-effort healthy grocery cart' for the household.
author_notes: Expert. Food claim vs pharmacology evidence.
